meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve no version control (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Antigravity Template for Version Control
content.seo_description: Master version control for freelance projects. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to standardize your development workflow and eliminate deployment risks.
content.answer_block: The absence of version control is a ticking time bomb for freelance profitability. By implementing the Antigravity project template, you standardize directory structures and automate git initialization. Combined with Claude Code, this workflow enforces strict commit hygiene, ensuring every client project is production-ready, reversible, and professional from the very first line of code.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: a client requests a 'quick change,' you overwrite the only working version of the codebase, and suddenly, you're debugging at 3 AM. For the modern freelancer, lack of version control isn't just a technical oversight—it’s a threat to your reputation and your bottom line. 

Stop treating your projects like disposable prototypes. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you move from chaotic hacking to systematic engineering. This approach provides a robust architectural foundation, allowing you to ship features with the speed of an agentic workflow and the reliability of a seasoned architect.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Standardize your project structure immediately to ensure git-ready architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the 'Standard-SaaS' Antigravity template. Ensure it includes a .gitignore, .env.example, and a structured /src directory.

steps.title: Automate Version Control Setup
steps.description: Ensure every project starts with a git repository and a clean 'initial commit' state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the current project to automatically initialize a git repository and create a 'chore: initial project scaffold' commit.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'chore: initial project scaffold'

steps.title: Implement Semantic Commit Hooking
steps.description: Use Claude Code to enforce semantic commit messages, making your history readable for future maintenance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a git hook that validates commit messages against the Conventional Commits specification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'add a husky pre-commit hook to enforce conventional commits'

steps.title: Modular Architecture Migration
steps.description: Refactor existing loose files into the Antigravity modular structure for better isolation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current codebase into the Antigravity feature-based module structure, ensuring clear separation of concerns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'move existing logic into /src/modules based on the Antigravity structure'

steps.title: Automated Deployment Verification
steps.description: Bridge the gap between code changes and deployment with automated checks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD workflow file that runs linting and unit tests before allowing a merge to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'create a .github/workflows/ci.yml file that runs npm test on push'

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file in your template; it prevents 'missing environment variable' bugs when moving between local and production.
Map your features to specific git branches to allow client-specific experimental testing without affecting the main production branch.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation from your code comments; it keeps your README.md synchronized with your logic changes.
Implement a 'rollback' script in your package.json that allows you to revert to the last stable release state in one command.
Treat your project template as a product; iterate on your Antigravity config every time you find a recurring manual task.
content.next_steps: Once your template is live, create a private repository for your 'master' version and use it as a boilerplate for all future freelance client projects.
content.conversion_block: Stop hacking and start engineering. With the right template, you save hours per project and build client trust that leads to referrals. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
